name: Albalat dels Tarongers Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona - Circular por Sender Albalat dels Tarongers (PR-CV 318)
description: Hike the moderate 8.0-mile Albalat dels Tarongers Trail in Serra Calderona Natural Park, gaining 1398 feet over 4 hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Albalat Dels Tarongers offers diverse landscapes within the Camp de Morvedre region, partly encompassed by the Serra Calderona Natural Park. The area features prominent mountains like Montalt (427 meters) and El Garbí (593 meters), alongside the Palància River valley. The terrain includes extensive pine and cork oak forests, as well as agricultural areas with orange groves. Best hiking trails around Albalat Dels Tarongers The most popular hiking route is The route of the Garbí chains - Circular from Segart, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete, offering views of the Serra Calderona. Another top favourite among local hikers is Albalat dels Tarongers Trail, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including forested sections and open landscapes. Local hikers also love the El Pi del Salt – Mola de Segart loop from el Xocainet, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail leading through mountain scenery, often completed in about 4 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Albalat Dels Tarongers is defined by pine and cork oak forests, mountain peaks with viewpoints, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Albalat Dels Tarongers are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Albalat Dels Tarongers's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Albalat dels Tarongers?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes around Albalat dels Tarongers, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find options from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, with 9 moderate and 7 difficult trails to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Albalat dels Tarongers?
A: The terrain around Albalat dels Tarongers is quite varied. You can expect to hike through extensive pine and cork oak forests, agricultural areas with orange groves, and along the Palància River valley. Many routes also feature mountainous sections, including prominent peaks like Montalt and El Garbí, offering scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Albalat dels Tarongers?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy trail suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous walk. The region's diverse landscape, including the Vía Verde Ojos Negros, also offers gentler paths that can be enjoyed by all ages.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Albalat dels Tarongers?
A: The best times to hike in Albalat dels Tarongers are typically spring and autumn when the temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters are generally mild and suitable for hiking.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Albalat dels Tarongers offers several circular routes. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e816845654/the-route-of-the-garbi-chains-circular-from-segart" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The route of the Garbí chains - Circular from Segart&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pi del Salt – Mola de Segart loop from el Xocainet&lt;/a&gt; also provides a rewarding circular experience through mountain scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Hikers can discover a wealth of natural features and landmarks. Highlights include the stunning Garbí Viewpoint, offering panoramic vistas, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4079174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albalat dels Tarongers Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also home to the Cova del Aigua Amarga (Cova de La Cabra), a cave with archaeological significance, and the picturesque Palància River valley.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Albalat dels Tarongers?
A: The hiking routes in Albalat dels Tarongers are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths within the Serra Calderona Natural Park, and the rewarding views from mountain peaks like El Garbí.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation gain or challenging ascents?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are 7 difficult trails with significant elevation gain. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33744845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Garbí Scramble – Canal del Garbí Summit loop from Segart&lt;/a&gt; involve substantial ascents, leading to rewarding views from peaks such as El Garbí.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options directly to trailheads may vary, Albalat dels Tarongers is a municipality within the Camp de Morvedre region. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional train connections to nearby towns, and then plan for a short taxi ride or walk to the starting points of the trails.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can explore the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4079174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albalat dels Tarongers Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive panoramic views. Additionally, the Cova del Aigua Amarga (Cova de La Cabra) is a natural feature with archaeological significance, featuring cave art dating back to 2000 BC.

Q: What is the longest hiking trail in the area?
A: One of the longer routes you can undertake is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pi del Salt – Mola de Segart loop from el Xocainet&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 16.3 kilometers (10.1 miles) and typically takes around 5 hours to complete, leading through beautiful mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the Serra Calderona Natural Park?
A: Yes, a significant portion of Albalat dels Tarongers lies within the Serra Calderona Natural Park, meaning many trails traverse its beautiful pine and cork oak forests. This park is known for its emblematic scenery and extensive network of paths.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Albalat dels Tarongers Trail?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular route usually takes around 4 hours and 6 to 10 minutes to complete. However, your actual hiking time may vary depending on your pace, stops for breaks, and how much time you spend enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Albalat dels Tarongers Trail is graded as a moderate hike. It involves a significant elevation gain and loss of around 426 meters over its nearly 13-kilometer length, making it suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is the Albalat dels Tarongers Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop and brings you back to your starting point without retracing your steps. It's officially known as the PR-CV 318 "Circular route by Sender Albalat dels Tarongers".

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied landscapes characteristic of the Serra Calderona Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of paths, including sections like the "camí dels Terrers" and "camí del Garbí," ascending the Barranc de Pujol, and connecting with the "camí de la Gronsa." Expect natural features like the Font de la Murta and the well-preserved Barranc de les Merles, along with Mediterranean forests and mountain views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the trail, you can discover several notable spots. These include the historic Albalat dels Tarongers Castle, the refreshing Font de la Murta, and the summit of El Xocainet. You'll also pass through well-preserved areas like the Barranc de les Merles and enjoy views from the open refuge of Morró.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The region around Valencia, where this trail is located, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making the moderate difficulty more enjoyable. Summers can be hot, and winters might be cooler and wetter, so check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any specific navigation challenges or tips for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's important to be aware that the trail's signage has known deficiencies. Both horizontal (paint markers) and vertical (directional posts and information panels) signs may be inadequate or lacking. We highly recommend using a reliable GPS device or a digital map, such as the komoot app, to navigate the route safely and ensure you stay on track.

faqs.question: Is the Albalat dels Tarongers Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Serra Calderona Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Serra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the Serra Calderona Natural Park or to hike the Albalat dels Tarongers Trail within it. However, always respect park regulations, especially regarding waste, fire, and wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Albalat dels Tarongers Trail is not detailed in our current data. However, as the trail starts and ends in Albalat dels Tarongers, you should look for public parking options within the town itself. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Albalat dels Tarongers Trail (Sendero Albalat dels Tarongers) is the primary route, but it also shares sections with other paths. You might find yourself on parts of the Camí de la Gronsa, Camí de la Rodana, Camí de les Merles, and Camí del Garbí, among others, as you explore the area.
